policy: Exact reviewed fingerprints only. Any new, changed, or stale result fails CI and requires security review.

entries.reason: The logged value is a minimum password-length policy number embedded in a bootstrap validation error, not a credential or secret.
Security regression fixture in test-only code; it is not shipped as an application sanitizer.

Browser sessions use Secure SameSite=Lax cookies and credentialed cross-origin API requests use an explicit origin allowlist. CodeQL does not model these controls through cookie-parser and the custom session layer.
Historical-version access requires an immutable citation receipt matching user, program, document, version, and source index; the query parameters do not independently authorize access.
Static fingerprinted assets and the SPA shell are bounded file reads, not sensitive API operations. API abuse controls are applied in route-specific middleware and handlers.
Reviewed risk-tiered route baseline; authorization and bounded request inputs exist, while universal route quotas remain tracked hardening work.

The ask path uses shared PostgreSQL per-user and per-program fixed-window limits; CodeQL does not recognize the custom limiter.
Login and password-reset routes use bounded in-memory per-IP and per-email limiters; CodeQL does not recognize the custom limiter.
Reviewed risk-tiered route baseline; authorization, file-size limits, and bounded inputs exist, while universal route quotas remain tracked hardening work.
Reviewed read/write route baseline; authorization, classification, and program scoping exist, while universal route quotas remain tracked hardening work.
Reviewed authenticated profile-read baseline; universal route quotas remain tracked hardening work.
OIDC uses signed state, PKCE, nonce, issuer/audience validation, and a short-lived flow cookie; universal route quotas remain tracked hardening work.
Reviewed authenticated session-management baseline; authorization exists, while universal route quotas remain tracked hardening work.
